gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
gettingStarted.description: Everything you need to start using MagellAIn on the water.

items.title: Creating Your Account
items.content: Sign up with your email, or use Google or Apple sign-in on the sign-in screen. Once in, go to Menu > My Boats to add your primary boat. MagellAIn uses your boat details to personalize AI coaching, performance analytics, and race data to your specific setup.

items.title: Adding Your Boat
items.content: Go to Menu > My Boats > Add Boat. Enter your boat's name, class (e.g. J/70, S2 7.9, Cal 25), sail number, and PHRF rating if applicable. MagellAIn uses this to tailor performance analytics and AI coaching to your specific boat. You can add multiple boats if you race on different vessels.

items.title: Your Home Dashboard
items.content: The home screen is your at-a-glance command center. It shows an AI-generated sailing conditions summary (rated Excellent, Good, Fair, Marginal, or Poor) with tips for the day, current wind and wave data from NOAA and NDBC buoys, your next scheduled race from FYC or WSSC, your primary boat details, quick-access buttons for Chart, Weather, AI Coach, and GPS Speed, and a live sailing news feed pulling recent articles from Sail Magazine, Scuttlebutt, World Sailing, and Sailing World. Sign in to also see your personalized boat and race data.

items.title: Navigating the App
items.content: MagellAIn uses a bottom navigation bar designed for one-handed use on the water. The five main tabs are: Home (dashboard with conditions summary, next race, quick actions, and news feed), Map (nautical charts, GPS tracking, race courses, and race tools), Route (AI-powered passage planning with weather routing), Coach (AI sailing coach with voice input), and Menu (profile, boats, performance, races, safety, settings, and help). The MagellAIn wordmark on the home screen taps back to the home dashboard.

mapAndCharts.title: Map and Charts
mapAndCharts.description: Interactive nautical charts with live data overlays, GPS tracking, track replay, race courses, and tactical analysis.

items.title: Using the Map
items.content: The map centers on Ford Yacht Club / Detroit River by default. Pinch to zoom, drag to pan. The NOAA chart overlay shows depths, channel markers, aids to navigation, and hazards. Map controls are in the top-left (Layers, Reset view, Refresh weather). Your GPS speed displays as a compact chip at the top-center. All race tools — Courses, GPS tracking, Race Timer, Wind Shifts, and Start Line Bias — are accessed from the tab bar at the bottom of the map. Tap any tab (Courses, GPS, Timer, Tools) to open the corresponding panel. Tap the tab again, or tap anywhere on the map above the panel, to dismiss it. Wind Shift and Start Line Bias panels open from the Tools tab and slide up above the tab bar.

items.title: Pre-Race Start Timer
items.content: Tap the Timer tab in the bottom tab bar on the map to open the Race Timer panel. Tap '5 MIN' to begin the standard 5-minute US Sailing starting sequence — the timer counts down through Warning (yellow), Prep (orange), and One Minute (red) phases, then switches to a racing elapsed clock when the gun fires. Tap 'SYNC' instead when you hear the actual race committee gun — the timer immediately starts your racing clock from that moment. Audio signals play at each phase transition: three beeps at Warning and Prep, six rapid beeps at One Minute, and a long horn tone at the start. Tap Pause to freeze the countdown and resume with Play. Tap Reset to return to idle. The timer keeps running even when you switch to a different tab.

items.title: Wind Arrow Overlay
items.content: Toggle the wind arrow overlay from the Layers panel in the top-left map controls. When on, colored directional arrows appear at each NDBC station, showing observed wind direction and speed. Arrow color indicates intensity: green for light air, yellow for moderate breeze, orange for fresh, and red for strong winds. Arrow size grows with wind speed. Tap any arrow to see a popup with station name, exact wind speed in knots, compass direction, and gust speed if available. Arrows update whenever weather data refreshes.

items.title: Live Buoy Data
items.content: Tap any NDBC buoy marker on the map to see real-time observations: wind speed and direction, wave height and period, water temperature, and air pressure. Year-round shore stations include Toledo Harbor (THRO1), South Bass Island (SBIO1), and Marblehead OH (MRHO1). Seasonal offshore buoys — Toledo Channel Light (45132), W Lake Erie Buoy (45005), and Cleveland Buoy (45142) — deploy in late spring and show 'Seasonal buoy — not yet deployed' before then. Data refreshes automatically every few minutes.

items.title: Race Course Selection
items.content: Tap the Courses tab in the bottom tab bar to open the course selection panel. It lists all active race courses from Ford YC and WSSC, with course type (windward-leeward, triangle, etc.) and total distance in nautical miles. Tap any course to select it and see its legs — each leg shows the mark name, rounding direction (port or starboard), bearing, and distance. Toggle 'Show course marks on map' to display racing marks as colored pins on the chart. Select a course and tap 'AI Tactical Analysis' to get leg-by-leg tactical guidance based on current wind conditions.

items.title: AI Tactical Analysis
items.content: After selecting a race course in the Courses panel, tap the 'AI Tactical Analysis' button to open the tactical analysis overlay. For each leg of the course it shows the leg bearing (true degrees and compass direction), leg distance in nautical miles, your point of sail (Close Hauled, Beam Reach, Broad Reach, Running, etc.) based on current NDBC wind direction, and specific tactical advice tailored to conditions — for example: 'Hike hard, depower with traveler/backstay in 18 knots upwind' or 'Fly the spinnaker if available on the broad reach.' The analysis updates with live wind data.

items.title: GPS Track Recording
items.content: Tap the GPS tab in the bottom tab bar to open the GPS & Track panel. Tap 'Start' to begin recording your track — your device GPS logs position, speed, and heading continuously. Live stats show elapsed time, current speed in knots, total distance in nautical miles, and max speed. When finished, tap 'Stop' then 'Save' to store the track. Saved tracks appear in the Track Replay list in the same panel and sync to your account when signed in, or save locally if you are offline or not signed in.

items.title: Track Replay
items.content: Tap the GPS tab in the bottom tab bar to open the GPS & Track panel, then scroll down to Track Replay. It lists all your saved tracks with date, duration, distance, and max speed. Tap a track to load it — the full route draws on the map in orange. Use the playback controls to Play, Pause, or Stop the replay. A pulsing dot moves along the route showing your position at each recorded moment. Choose from 1x, 5x, 20x, or 50x playback speed. Tap the X icon to return to the track list.

items.title: Speedometer and GPS Speed
items.content: MagellAIn automatically starts GPS speed tracking when you open the map — no button press needed. Your current speed appears as a compact pill at the top-center of the map, alongside a compass arrow showing heading. Tap the pill to cycle through knots, mph, and km/h. The speedometer disappears if your device denies GPS permission. You can also mention your current speed in the AI Coach chat for real-time trim advice.

items.title: Start Line Bias Tool
items.content: Tap the Tools tab in the bottom tab bar, then tap 'Start Line Bias' to open the tool. The panel slides up above the tab bar. Place the two ends of your start line: tap 'Boat / Pin End,' then tap the map where the pin end of the line is; next tap 'Committee' and tap the committee boat position. The tool calculates the bias angle — how many degrees the line deviates from perpendicular to the wind — and tells you which end is favored and by how many degrees. The line draws on the chart in amber with a green circle at the boat end and blue square at the committee end. Bias under 2° is a square line. Wind direction is pulled automatically from the nearest active NDBC station. Tap 'Clear Line' to reset and re-mark. Use this in the 5-15 minutes before your start.

items.title: Wind Shift Graph (Map)
items.content: Tap the Tools tab in the bottom tab bar, then tap 'Wind Shifts' to open the Wind Shift panel. It shows the last 90 minutes of wind direction changes from any active NDBC shore station. Use the dropdown to switch between stations. The graph plots wind direction over time, and the summary line tells you if the wind is veering (shifting clockwise — S to SW to W) or backing (shifting counter-clockwise). Veering wind in the northern hemisphere often indicates an approaching warm front. This chart is also available on the Weather page as 'Wind Shift History' (shown near the top of the weather screen for quick access). Data from the NDBC realtime observation feed, updated every 10-30 minutes.

items.title: Layline Overlay
items.content: Enable laylines from the Layers panel (tap the Layers icon in the top-left map controls, then check 'Laylines'). Laylines appear as dashed lines extending from the windward mark — green for port tack, red for starboard tack. They show you exactly where to tack to fetch the mark on either tack. The tacking angle slider (30–55°, default 42°) lets you tune the calculation to your boat and conditions: a flat-water J/70 in 15 knots might tack in 40°, while a heavy-displacement PHRF boat in chop might be 48°. Laylines require a race course to be selected in the Courses panel and live wind data from an active NDBC station. They update automatically when wind direction changes. Note: laylines are theoretical — always account for tide, current, and pressure bands in actual sailing.

items.title: Pre-Race Checklist
items.content: The Pre-Race Checklist is on the Races page (Menu > Races). It appears as a collapsible card at the top of the page, above the regatta schedules. The checklist has five sections: Rig (mast, rigging, running gear), Sails (halyards, battens, telltales, spinnaker), Safety (PFDs, horn, throwable, flares, VHF), Boat (bung, bilge, hatches, fire extinguisher), and Race Day (protest flag, class flag, timer sync, course number, crew brief, start plan). Tap any category header to expand it, then tap individual items to check them off. A progress bar tracks overall readiness. Tap 'Reset' to clear all checks before the next race, or 'Check All' to mark everything done. Checklist state is session-only and resets when you reload the app.

routePlanner.title: Route Planner
routePlanner.description: AI-powered passage planning with weather routing and boat polar performance optimization.

items.title: Route Planner Overview
items.content: The Route tab in the bottom navigation opens the AI-powered route planner. Select a pre-loaded Great Lakes passage (Lake St. Clair Circuit, Ford YC to Lake Erie, Detroit River to Put-in-Bay, Port Huron to Mackinac, or others), choose your boat, and set a departure time. MagellAIn computes the optimal route using real-time NDBC weather observations and your boat's polar performance data.

items.title: Computing a Route
items.content: Select a passage from the dropdown, choose your boat (boats with PHRF ratings are listed), set your departure date and time, then tap 'Compute Route.' The planner fetches current weather observations from nearby NDBC stations, interpolates wind conditions along each leg using your boat's polar data, and calculates optimal speed, sail selection, and estimated duration for each leg. Results include total distance, estimated duration, average speed, ETA, and a comparison to the straight rhumb-line route.

items.title: Understanding Route Results
items.content: After computation, you see a Route Summary card with key metrics (distance, duration, average speed, ETA), a risk assessment (low/moderate/high/extreme based on wind strength and passage duration), recommended sail changes between legs, and expandable leg-by-leg details showing distance, bearing, true wind angle, boat speed, and sail type for each leg. The weather data section shows how many observations were used. For best results, compute routes close to your actual departure time when weather data is freshest.

weather.title: Weather and Wind
weather.description: Detailed marine forecasts interpreted through a sailing lens.

items.title: Weather Dashboard
items.content: The Weather page (accessible via Home screen quick actions or Menu) shows: a sailing conditions summary (Excellent through Poor) with tactical tips; a Wind Shift History chart (90 minutes of wind direction from any active NDBC station — shown near the top so you see trends immediately); then current observations from all active NDBC stations in the area; and finally multi-day marine forecasts by zone. Tap Refresh to pull the latest data from NOAA.

items.title: Understanding Wind Data
items.content: Wind is displayed in knots with direction shown as both degrees and compass abbreviation (e.g. 'ESE'). Color-coded severity bands map to Beaufort scale categories — green for calm/light air, yellow for breeze, orange for fresh, red for strong/gale. Data is sourced from NDBC buoy observations at shore stations and offshore buoys. Offshore buoys (45005 W Lake Erie, 45132 Toledo Channel Light, 45142 Cleveland) are seasonal and show 'Not yet deployed' in early spring — they typically deploy in May.

items.title: Weather Alerts
items.content: MagellAIn monitors active NOAA weather alerts for your sailing area. Small Craft Advisories, Gale Warnings, and Thunderstorm Warnings appear as prominent amber or red banners on the Home and Weather screens. These alerts also feed into the AI coach, which can help you interpret conditions and decide whether to sail.

items.title: Wind Shift History
items.content: The Weather page includes a Wind Shift History section near the top, right after the sailing conditions summary. Select any shore station using the dropdown, then view a chart of wind direction over the last 90 minutes. A summary line indicates whether the wind is veering (clockwise) or backing (counter-clockwise) and by how many degrees. Tap Refresh to reload the latest NDBC data. The same chart is accessible from the Map screen via the Tools tab (tap 'Wind Shifts').

items.title: Station Observations
items.content: Each buoy station card shows wind speed and gust, wave height (where available), air temperature, water temperature, and barometric pressure. Year-round shore stations include Toledo Harbor (THRO1), South Bass Island OH (SBIO1), and Marblehead OH (MRHO1). Seasonal offshore buoys — Toledo Channel Light (45132), W Lake Erie Buoy (45005), and Cleveland Buoy (45142) — become available once deployed in spring. Marine forecasts below the station cards cover the Detroit River/Lake St. Clair corridor (LEZ045) and Western Lake Erie open water zones.

aiCoach.title: AI Sailing Coach
aiCoach.description: Your personal Claude-powered sailing intelligence assistant with voice input and live conditions context.

items.title: Talking to Your Coach
items.content: The Coach tab in the bottom navigation connects you to MagellAIn's AI sailing coach, powered by Claude. Ask questions in plain English about racing tactics, sail trim, weather interpretation, rules, or boat handling. The coach knows your boat type, your waters, and current conditions, so answers are specific and actionable rather than generic.

items.title: Streaming Responses with Live Weather
items.content: The AI coach streams responses in real time — words appear as they are generated rather than arriving all at once. This means you start reading useful information within a second or two of sending a question. The coach also has access to live current conditions from the NDBC buoys, so if you ask what the wind is doing right now, it will reference actual observed readings from the nearest active station.

items.title: Voice Input
items.content: Tap the microphone button to the left of the text input to speak your question hands-free. A pulsing indicator shows when the coach is listening. Speak naturally — your words appear in the input field as you talk. When you stop speaking, the text is ready to review and send. Voice input requires microphone permission in your browser settings. Available on most modern mobile browsers.

items.title: What You Can Ask
items.content: The AI coach handles a wide range of sailing topics: pre-race strategy ('Wind is 8 knots out of the SW, which end of the line should I start?'), rules questions ('Boat on port tack crossed me at the windward mark, do I have rights?'), trim advice ('My J/70 is slow upwind in 12-15 knots, what should I check?'), weather interpretation ('Is this frontal passage going to shift the wind?'), post-race analysis ('I finished 4th — what should I work on?'), and course tactics ('We're sailing Course B today with a WSW wind, what's the plan?').

items.title: Context-Aware Coaching
items.content: The AI coach automatically factors in current weather conditions from your area, your boat's specifications and PHRF rating, and local knowledge about Lake Erie and Detroit River sailing patterns. The more boat and conditions information you share in the conversation, the more specific and useful the advice becomes. For tactical analysis on a specific course, use the map's AI Tactical Analysis panel first, then bring your questions to the coach.

items.title: Chat History
items.content: Your conversation with the AI coach is kept active while the Coach tab is open. Navigating away and returning within the same browser session will usually restore your messages, but a full page reload starts fresh. Tap the trash icon in the top-right corner of the Coach screen to clear the conversation and start a new one at any time.

racing.title: Race Management
racing.description: Schedules, results, performance analytics, and race result import for your racing.

items.title: Race Schedules
items.content: Go to Menu > Races to see upcoming and past events from Ford Yacht Club, West Shore Sail Club, and DRYA series — including Wednesday Night at the Races (WNATR), weekend regattas, and championship series. The Pre-Race Checklist card appears at the top of this screen. Tap any regatta to expand it and see race-by-race results and series standings.

items.title: Race Results and Standings
items.content: Tap any regatta to expand it. If results have been entered, you'll see each race row with status, course type, and wind. Expand a race to see the full fleet result with boat names, sail numbers, positions, and elapsed/corrected times. Series standings are computed automatically from results within the regatta, showing points and place per fleet. Export standings as a PDF using the PDF button.

items.title: Importing Race Results from CSV
items.content: Tap the 'Import' button on the Races screen (top-right) to open the CSV import wizard. The four-step wizard guides you through: (1) Upload — select a CSV file exported from YachtScoring, PHRF-LE, or any spreadsheet. (2) Column Mapping — the app auto-detects your column headers and maps sail number, boat name, elapsed time, corrected time, and finish position. (3) Select Race — choose an existing regatta and race number, or create a new one. (4) Preview and Confirm — review all entries before saving. Results are saved to your account and immediately appear in the Performance screen.

items.title: Performance Analytics
items.content: Menu > Performance shows your full race history for your primary boat. Key stats at the top: total races, wins, podiums, average finishing position, and average VMG. The Season Trend chart plots your position across up to 20 recent races. Below that, Series Results show your standings in each active regatta. The Race Log lists every race in reverse chronological order — tap any row to expand the full fleet result. Tap the PDF button to export your complete race log and analytics.

items.title: Head-to-Head Competitor Records
items.content: The Performance screen includes a Head-to-Head section showing all competitors you've raced against in the same fleet. For each boat you'll see: number of shared races, your win/loss record, and the PHRF time correction advantage or disadvantage in seconds per mile. A green/red win-rate bar gives a quick visual of your competitive record. This helps you identify your closest rivals and understand where you win or lose on corrected time.

safety.title: Safety Features
safety.description: Emergency tools and safety planning for on-water confidence.

items.title: Emergency Assist
items.content: The Emergency page is accessible from Menu > Emergency. It provides one-tap calling to 911 and direct numbers for USCG Sector Detroit (313-568-9564) and USCG Station Toledo (419-691-4048). Tap 'Get My Location' to display your GPS coordinates in large text for relaying to rescue services. The page also includes safety reminders about PFD use, cold water risk, and VHF Channel 16 monitoring.

items.title: SOS Text to Emergency Contacts
items.content: After getting your location on the Emergency page, tap any saved emergency contact to open a pre-filled SMS on your phone with your GPS coordinates and a Google Maps link. Tap Add on the Emergency page to save personal emergency contacts (name, phone, relationship). Contacts sync to your account. You can also set a primary emergency contact in Menu > Profile.

items.title: Float Plans
items.content: File a float plan before heading out through Menu > Float Plan. Enter your departure location, destination, expected return time, and number of persons aboard. Your vessel details are pre-filled from your boat profile. Shore contact information can be added for someone who should alert authorities if you do not return on time. Active plans are saved and viewable in the Active Plans tab until you close them after returning to dock.

items.title: Weather Safety Alerts
items.content: Active NOAA weather alerts for your sailing area appear as prominent banners on the Home and Weather screens whenever a Small Craft Advisory, Gale Warning, or Thunderstorm Warning is in effect. The AI coach also references active alerts in its responses so you can make informed go/no-go decisions.

offlineMode.title: Offline Mode
offlineMode.description: MagellAIn keeps working even when you lose connectivity on the water.

items.title: How Offline Mode Works
items.content: MagellAIn uses a service worker to cache app pages and recently loaded data so the app continues to function when you lose signal on the water. All main screens remain accessible offline with cached content. An amber banner appears at the top of the screen automatically when your device goes offline, and disappears when connection is restored.

items.title: GPS Tracking Offline
items.content: GPS track recording works fully offline — your device's GPS does not need an internet connection. The always-on GPS speedometer also works offline. Tracks recorded while offline are saved to your device's local storage and will sync to your account automatically the next time you open the app with a connection.

items.title: What Data Is Available Offline
items.content: Weather data, race schedules, and AI coach conversations require an internet connection to refresh. However, the most recently loaded weather data, your saved race results, and your saved GPS tracks remain accessible from cache when offline. NOAA weather and buoy data is cached for up to one hour. Course data, performance records, and help content are also available offline once initially loaded.

items.title: Offline Page
items.content: If MagellAIn cannot load a page you have not visited before due to no connection, you will see the offline page with a prompt to return to home. Navigate to any recently visited screen and your cached data will be available.

settings.title: Settings and Preferences
settings.description: Customize MagellAIn to match how you sail.

items.title: Speed Units
items.content: The GPS speedometer chip on the map cycles through knots, mph, and km/h when tapped. Wind and boat speed units elsewhere default to knots. Knots is the standard nautical unit recommended for racing.

items.title: Display Theme
items.content: Switch between Light, Dark, and System themes under Menu > Settings > Appearance. Dark mode is recommended for use on the water as it reduces glare and improves readability in bright sunlight. The System setting automatically matches your device's current display mode.

items.title: Your Profile
items.content: Go to Menu > Profile to update your display name, home club, and emergency contact information. Your profile details help the AI coach calibrate responses to your experience level and local sailing area. Keeping your emergency contact current is important for the SOS feature.

items.title: Changing Your Password
items.content: If you signed up with email and password, you can change your password under Menu > Settings > Change Password. Enter your new password twice to confirm. If you signed in with Google or Apple, your password is managed by those accounts and cannot be changed here.
